schema_version.description: Schema version of the artifact; bumped on breaking ABI changes.

task_id.description: Task id this contract belongs to.

root.description: Absolute path to the project repository root.

base_ref.description: Git ref the run is based on.

base_sha.description: Resolved base commit SHA, when known.

dirty_policy.description: How a dirty working tree is handled when creating an envelope: snapshot captures the dirty state as the base SHA without touching the live tree; refuse fails loudly.

repo.description: Repository the run operates on and how a dirty tree is handled.

kind.description: Canonical run mode: ask and plan are read-only, agent writes. Engine strategies (race width, attempts, deep_scan, create, delegate) are flags on a mode, not modes.

mode.description: Canonical run mode for this task.

normalized.description: Optional normalized restatement of the prompt.

user_intent.description: The user's request this run is held to.

instructions.description: Caller-supplied per-run system-level instructions for task-producing lanes.

output_schema.description: Normalized caller-supplied JSON Schema for the final answer; null when the run has no structured-output contract.

auth_preference.description: Requested auth route for the run (subscription/api_key/auto); the telemetry auth_route receipt carries the effective truth.

credential_profile_id.description: Requested credential profile for the run; null = engine-default credentials (INV-135 route evidence).

max_turns.description: Per-run turn cap (beats per-harness settings); null when the caller set none.

protected_paths.description: Approval globs — path globs (e.g. `migrations/**`, `**/*.env`) whose changes escalate a run to a human-approval gate before it can be applied. Spec/config-owned and never suppressed by per-run protected-path approvals.

deny_paths.description: Per-run globs no candidate may touch at all; enforced by the engine post-diff gate on envelope runs (in-place runs with deny_paths are refused). accept_risk may still deliver (INV-111).

auto_protected_paths.description: Engine-derived gate/test path protections; per-run approvals can narrow only this set.

path.description: Glob approved by the operator/user for this run; consumed only by the auto-protected gate/test path policy, not by built-in critical-path gates.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason for the approval.

items.description: Per-run typed operator approval allowing changes under an auto-protected gate/test path.

protected_path_approvals.description: Per-run typed approvals for protected gate/test path changes, produced by explicit surfaces and consumed by policy.

constraints.description: Path-protection constraints applied to a run.

program.description: Executable invoked directly without an implicit shell.

args.description: Exact argv passed to the executable.

cwd.description: Optional project-relative working directory for the command.

envAllowlist.description: Non-secret parent environment names explicitly forwarded to the command.

required.description: Whether this test must pass (vs advisory).

trust_required.description: Whether this command originated in versioned project config.

projectDigest.description: Digest of the canonical project identity.

configDigest.description: Digest of the parsed versioned project config blob.

commandDigest.description: Digest of the canonical command invocation.

executablePath.description: Resolved executable path approved by the operator.

executableDigest.description: Digest of the resolved executable bytes.

anyOf.description: Content hash in algo:hex form, e.g. "sha256:abcd...".

accessProfile.description: Effective access profile covered by this grant.

anyOf.description: External exact grant for one versioned project test command.

trust_grant.description: Matching external grant; null when none was found.

items.description: A deterministic typed-argv test command used as a verification gate.

commands.description: Deterministic test commands configured as gates.

tests.description: Deterministic test gates for the run.

effective_profile.description: Access profile actually enforced by the engine (mode/trust clamps applied; never client-supplied).

web_required.description: Whether the task requires live web evidence.

effective_mode.description: Policy the selected route actually executes (disclosed upgrades, e.g. cached to live).

external_context.description: External web/context policy for the run.

web.description: Web policy forwarded to tool permissioning.

tool_permission_policy.description: Tool permission policy passed to harnesses that support allow/deny lists.

routing_goal.description: Routing objective: pace expiring quota, choose the highest declared quality tier, or minimize incremental paid spend.

paid_budget.description: Incremental-cash budget: explicitly unlimited or a finite non-negative USD cap.

budget.description: Routing goal and explicit incremental-cash budget for the run.

routing_models.description: Resolved harness-scoped model map for this run (harness id to model id); empty means every route uses its per-harness settings default.

additionalProperties.description: Cross-harness reasoning-effort level, weakest to strongest; adapters declare the subset they support and a shared normalizer clamps requests onto the nearest supported level.

routing_efforts.description: Resolved harness-scoped reasoning-effort map for this run (harness id to effort), frozen so an Exact Retry replays the same effort instead of re-resolving current settings.

require_tests_pass.description: Require all configured test commands to pass before convergence.

require_no_accepted_block_open.description: Block convergence while an accepted BLOCK review finding is still open.

require_no_accepted_fix_first_open.description: Block convergence while an accepted FIX_FIRST review finding is still open.

require_final_cross_family_clean_review.description: Require a final clean review from a different provider family before convergence.

require_final_diff_stable_after_review.description: Require the diff to be unchanged after the final review (a mutated diff makes the review stale).

require_no_accepted_needs_human_open.description: Block convergence while an accepted NEEDS_HUMAN escalation is still open.

convergence.description: Predicate deciding when an until-clean/convergence run may stop as successful.

TaskContract.description: Immutable contract describing a single run; built once, hashed, never mutated.
